questions.text: Who proposed the law of conservation of mass?
questions.explanation: Antoine Lavoisier proposed the law of conservation of mass which states that mass can neither be created nor destroyed in a chemical reaction.

questions.text: The atomic mass unit is denoted by 'u'.
questions.explanation: Atomic mass unit (amu) is denoted by 'u'. It is defined as one-twelfth the mass of a carbon-12 atom.

questions.correctAnswer: A molecule is a group of two or more atoms chemically bonded together.
questions.explanation: A molecule is the smallest particle of an element or compound that can exist independently. Examples: O₂, H₂O, CO₂.

questions.text: The chemical formula of water is ________.
questions.explanation: Water has the chemical formula H₂O, meaning each molecule contains 2 hydrogen atoms and 1 oxygen atom.
